2604.19656.title: Pause or Fabricate? Training Language Models for Grounded Reasoning
2604.19656.paper_summary: Large language models have achieved remarkable progress on complex reasoning tasks. However, they often implicitly fabricate information when inputs are incomplete, producing confident but unreliable conclusions -- a failure mode we term ungrounded reasoning. We argue that this issue arises not from insufficient reasoning capability, but from the lack of inferential boundary awareness -- the ability to recognize when the necessary premises for valid inference are missing. To address this issue, we propose Grounded Reasoning via Interactive Reinforcement Learning (GRIL), a multi-turn reinforcement learning framework for grounded reasoning under incomplete information. GRIL decomposes the reasoning process into two stages: clarify and pause, which identifies whether the available information is sufficient, and grounded reasoning, which performs task solving once the necessary premises are established. We design stage-specific rewards to penalize hallucinations, enabling models to detect gaps, stop proactively, and resume reasoning after clarification. Experiments on GSM8K-Insufficient and MetaMATH-Insufficient show that GRIL significantly improves premise detection (up to 45%), leading to a 30% increase in task success while reducing average response length by over 20%. Additional analyses confirm robustness to noisy user responses and generalization to out-of-distribution tasks.
2604.19656.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在複雜推理任務上取得了顯著的進展。<lb/>然而，當輸入不完整時，它們經常會隱性地編造信息，產生自信但不可靠的結論——這是一種我們稱之為無根據推理的失敗模式。<lb/>我們認為這個問題並不是由於推理能力不足，而是因為缺乏推理邊界意識——即識別有效推理所需前提缺失的能力。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了通過互動強化學習的有根據推理（GRIL），這是一個在不完整信息下進行有根據推理的多輪強化學習框架。<lb/>GRIL將推理過程分解為兩個階段：澄清和暫停，這一階段確定可用信息是否足夠，然後是有根據推理，當必要的前提確立後進行任務解決。<lb/>我們設計了階段特定的獎勵來懲罰幻覺，使模型能夠檢測空白，主動停止，並在澄清後恢復推理。<lb/>在GSM8K-不足和MetaMATH-不足的實驗中，GRIL顯著提高了前提檢測（高達45%），導致任務成功率提高30%，同時平均響應長度減少超過20%。<lb/>額外分析確認了對噪聲用戶響應的穩健性以及對分佈外任務的泛化能力。
2604.19656.author: Yiwen Qiu et.al.
2604.19656.authors: Yiwen Qiu, Linjuan Wu, Yizhou Liu, Yuchen Yan, Jin Ma, Xu Tan, Yao Hu, Daoxin Zhang, Wenqi Zhang, Weiming Lu, Jun Xiao, Yongliang Shen
